_doc: Hand-crafted reel script variants per DM keyword. Replaces the LLM-driven content engine — the Claude Code CLI was unreliable for batch JSON gen and burning subscription minutes. Each entry follows AIDA + Grand Slam Offer structure, no banned openers, no banned voice words, includes the literal 'Comment <KEYWORD>' CTA. The Content Engine selects variants in rotation, locks affiliate metadata to dm_keyword_registry.json, and ships. Add more variants per keyword as the funnel proves out — minimum 5 per live keyword to allow 5-day rotation without repetition.

LINK.hook: I had four years of bottles falling on my feet every morning.
LINK.beat1: The cabinet under the kitchen sink was the place things went to die. Sponges, dish soap, an entire bottle of expensive olive oil I forgot I owned. Every morning I reached for paper towels and knocked over six things.
LINK.turn: I got a Simple Houseware 2-tier sliding basket — chrome wire, holds 18 lbs per shelf, and the front rack slides out independently of the back.
LINK.result: Now I see everything. Sliding the front out doesn't disturb the back row. Haven't dropped a bottle in three weeks.

scenes.visual_prompt: first-person POV opening cluttered under-sink cabinet, bottles fall out
scenes.voiceover: I had four years of bottles falling on my feet.

scenes.visual_prompt: close-up of disorganized cleaning supplies, sponges piled, bottle on its side
scenes.voiceover: Reach for paper towels, knock over six things.

scenes.visual_prompt: frustrated person closing cabinet door quickly
scenes.voiceover: I'd given up. Just kept the door shut.

scenes.visual_prompt: Simple Houseware chrome 2-tier sliding basket organizer installed under sink, organized
scenes.voiceover: Then I got a 2-tier sliding basket. Chrome, 18 lb shelves.

scenes.visual_prompt: front basket slides forward smoothly while back row stays still, all items visible
scenes.voiceover: The front pulls out without disturbing the back row.

scenes.visual_prompt: person calmly grabbing item from organized cabinet, smiling
scenes.voiceover: Haven't dropped a bottle in three weeks.

LINK.hook: I avoided opening this cabinet for two whole years.
LINK.beat1: Every time I opened it I had to do a Tetris move just to grab dish soap. The wasted vertical space mocked me. There was room for two of everything if I could just get to it.
LINK.turn: The Simple Houseware 2-tier sliding basket fixed it in 4 minutes — no tools, no drilling, just sets on the cabinet floor. Each tier slides independently.
LINK.result: Doubled my usable storage without buying a single new container. The cabinet went from chaos to a system in one afternoon.

scenes.voiceover: Sliding basket fixed it in four minutes. No tools.

scenes.visual_prompt: both tiers slide out smoothly, all items immediately visible
scenes.voiceover: Doubled my storage with what I already owned.

scenes.visual_prompt: satisfied person calmly grabbing exactly what they need
scenes.voiceover: Comment LINK and I'll DM you the link.

LINK.hook: Three weeks ago this cabinet was where things went to die.
LINK.beat1: Open the door, hold your breath, hope you don't knock anything over. Sponges from a Costco run. Three half-empty bottles of the same cleaner. A measuring cup I lost track of in 2023.
LINK.turn: I added a Simple Houseware 2-tier chrome sliding basket. Holds 18 lbs per shelf. Sets directly on the cabinet floor — no installation.
LINK.result: Now everything has a row. The system tells me when I'm out of something because the slot is empty, not buried.

LINK.hook: The reason your cabinets stay messy is that nothing has a home.
LINK.beat1: Containers don't fix this. Bigger cabinets don't fix this. The problem is depth — when you can't reach the back without removing the front, the back becomes invisible.
LINK.turn: A sliding basket organizer fixes the depth problem. Simple Houseware's chrome 2-tier model holds 18 lbs per shelf — each tier pulls forward independently, so the back row is always one motion away.
LINK.result: Stopped buying duplicates of stuff I already owned. The cabinet became a system instead of a graveyard.

scenes.voiceover: Your cabinets are messy because nothing has a home.

scenes.voiceover: The real problem is depth, not size.

scenes.visual_prompt: back of cabinet revealed full of forgotten items
scenes.voiceover: If you can't reach the back, the back is invisible.

LINK.hook: Buying more containers will never fix your under-sink cabinet.
LINK.beat1: I tried clear bins. I tried stackable trays. I tried labeling everything. None of it solved the actual problem — that anything past the front 6 inches is functionally lost.
LINK.turn: What worked was a Simple Houseware 2-tier sliding basket. Chrome wire, 18 lbs per shelf, and the front rack slides independently to expose the back.
LINK.result: I threw out two bins of duplicate cleaners I'd bought because I forgot the first ones existed. The cabinet finally works.

PILLOW.hook: I had the wrong pillow for 10 years and didn't know it.
PILLOW.beat1: Woke up stiff every morning. Tried stacking two pillows. Tried rolling a towel under my neck. Switched sides. Bought a memory foam block that turned my head into a sweat puddle.
PILLOW.turn: Then I tried the Eli & Elm side sleeper pillow — U-shape cutout for your shoulder, latex-and-fiber blend you can adjust, and a cooling cover that actually stays cool.
PILLOW.result: First night I slept through. The morning crick is gone. My partner stole it the second night and I had to order another one.

scenes.visual_prompt: person waking up rubbing stiff neck, frustrated
scenes.voiceover: I had the wrong pillow for a decade.

scenes.visual_prompt: stack of two pillows on bed, towel rolled up next to it
scenes.voiceover: Stacked two pillows. Rolled towels under my neck.

scenes.visual_prompt: memory foam pillow with sweat stain
scenes.voiceover: Memory foam turned my head into a sweat puddle.

scenes.voiceover: Then I tried a U-shape side sleeper pillow.

scenes.visual_prompt: person sleeping peacefully on side, pillow contouring perfectly
scenes.voiceover: Slept through the first night. Crick is gone.

scenes.visual_prompt: second pillow being unboxed, partner had stolen the first
scenes.voiceover: Comment PILLOW and I'll DM you the link.

PILLOW.hook: I spent ten years thinking my mattress was the problem.
PILLOW.beat1: I bought a $1,400 mattress to fix my neck pain. Didn't help. Bought a topper. Didn't help. Saw a chiropractor twice a month for two years. Helped, but the pain came back every time.
PILLOW.turn: Turns out it was the pillow. Specifically the wrong pillow for a side sleeper. The Eli & Elm has a shoulder cutout that lets your shoulder drop into the bed instead of crushing it.
PILLOW.result: Three weeks of using it and the chronic stiffness is just gone. Saved myself another mattress.

scenes.visual_prompt: demonstration showing shoulder dropping into mattress beside pillow
scenes.voiceover: Your shoulder drops in instead of getting crushed.

scenes.visual_prompt: person sleeping peacefully, no neck strain
scenes.voiceover: Comment PILLOW and I'll DM you the link.

PILLOW.hook: Picture this: it is 4am, your neck won't turn left, and the alarm is in 90 minutes.
PILLOW.beat1: You stare at the ceiling. Your shoulder is numb. The pillow under your head is somehow both too high and too low. You flip it. Cold side, sure, but the angle is still wrong.
PILLOW.turn: An adjustable side sleeper pillow with a shoulder cutout fixes the angle problem. Eli & Elm — latex-fiber fill you can add or remove, U-shape that lets your shoulder rest naturally.
PILLOW.result: Same bed, same room, same body. The only thing that changed was the pillow. The 4am stare-at-the-ceiling routine just stopped.

scenes.voiceover: Adjustable U-shape pillow with shoulder cutout.

scenes.visual_prompt: shoulder resting naturally beside pillow cutout
scenes.voiceover: Shoulder rests naturally instead of compressing.

scenes.visual_prompt: morning light, person waking calm without pain
scenes.voiceover: Comment PILLOW and I'll DM you the link.

PILLOW.hook: Most pillows are designed for back sleepers. 74% of us sleep on our sides.
PILLOW.beat1: Standard pillows put your spine at a 30 degree angle when you sleep on your side. That's why you wake up feeling like someone twisted your neck while you were out.
PILLOW.turn: A side sleeper pillow keeps your spine straight. Eli & Elm uses a U-shape with a shoulder cutout and adjustable latex-fiber fill so you can dial in the height for your shoulder width.
PILLOW.result: Spine stays straight all night. Wake up the same way you fell asleep — relaxed, not crooked.

scenes.voiceover: Spine stays straight. Adjustable fill for your shoulder.

scenes.visual_prompt: morning, person stretches happily without pain
scenes.voiceover: Comment PILLOW and I'll DM you the link.

PILLOW.hook: Stop blaming your mattress for your neck pain.
PILLOW.beat1: Bought a fancy mattress. Bought a foam topper. Saw the chiro. The pain came back every time. The variable nobody changes is the pillow — same shape we've used for 70 years.
PILLOW.turn: A modern side sleeper pillow with a shoulder cutout actually addresses the geometry problem. Eli & Elm — adjustable latex-fiber fill, cooling cover, U-shape designed for the 74% of people who sleep on their side.
PILLOW.result: First week and the morning stiffness is just gone. Cost a fraction of a new mattress and fixed what the mattress couldn't.

HANGERS.hook: My closet had been a low-grade mess for longer than I want to admit.
HANGERS.beat1: I kept meaning to deal with it and kept not dealing with it. Things piled up, I could never find what I needed, and every time I opened it I just shut it again.
HANGERS.turn: Then I found a closet hangers sitting at 4.8 stars across 231,174 reviews, and it finally gave everything a place.
HANGERS.result: It runs about $36.84 and it's the first thing in a while that just worked. The closet actually stays sorted now.

scenes.voiceover: My closet had been a mess for longer than I'll admit.

scenes.voiceover: I kept meaning to deal with it and kept not dealing with it.

scenes.voiceover: The closet actually stays sorted now.
